title: glTF Shape Resource
description: Parameters describing a shape used for physics, BVH, or any other use case.

anyOf.description: An axis-aligned box with a size per-axis, centered at the origin in local space, with normals facing outwards along each axis.

A capsule, centered at the origin in local space with potentially different radii at each end, equivalent to the convex hull of two spheres located along the Y axis (in local space) at a specified distance, with normals facing outwards.

A cylinder, centered at the origin in local space with potentially different radii at each end, equivalent to the convex hull of two circles in the X/Z plane positioned along the Y axis at a specified distance, with the top cap normal facing along the +Y axis, the bottom cap normal facing along the -Y axis, and the side normals facing outwards along the X/Z plane.
A plane centered at the origin in local space, optionally with finite extents, with normal along the +Y axis in local space. The plane is one-sided.
A sphere, centered at the origin in local space with a radius equal in all directions, with normals facing outwards.

box.description: A set of parameter values that are used to define a box shape.

capsule.description: A set of parameter values that are used to define a capsule shape.

cylinder.description: A set of parameter values that are used to define a cylinder shape.

plane.description: A set of parameter values that are used to define a plane shape.

sphere.description: A set of parameter values that are used to define a sphere shape.
